question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / setIsAuthorized not scoped by action] Can an attacker authorized for victim call repayAndWithdrawCollateral with assets=0 (no repayment) and collateralWithdrawals=[all collateral], under the precondition that victim has no debt (so health check passes after withdrawal), triggering [Midnight.repay(market, 0, victim, ...) no-op -> Midnight.withdrawCollateral(market, idx, all_collateral, victim, attacker) -> isHealthy passes because debt=0], violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must require explicit owner action, causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's entire collateral with zero repayment using coarse authorization? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee / touchMarket interaction] Can the feeSetter set a market's settlement fee to the maximum allowed value (50 bps at 360d breakpoint) to implicitly cancel all low-price offers in that market, under the precondition that makers have signed offers with tick prices below the new settlement fee, triggering [feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee(id, 6, maxSettlementFee) -> Midnight.take for low-price buy offer reverts because buyerPrice < settlementFee -> bundle catches revert -> OutOfOffers], violating the invariant that valid signed offers must remain executable unless explicitly cancelled by the maker, causing scoped impact: feeSetter can implicitly cancel maker offers and DoS bundles by raising settlement fees?
